name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value larger than the original input

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The result of align_to_usize must itself be aligned to the given size

name: aligned_value_changes
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_value_changes(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: An already-aligned value must be a fixed point of align_to_usize

name: consecutive_both_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_consecutive_both_aligned(a: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        s >= 2,
        aligned(a, s),
    ensures
        aligned(a + 1, s),
{
}
reason: Two consecutive addresses cannot both be aligned to a stride of 2 or more

name: aligned_sum_nonaligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_sum_nonaligned(a: nat, b: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        s > 1,
        aligned(a, s),
        b > 0,
        b < s,
    ensures
        aligned(a + b, s),
{
}
reason: Adding a sub-stride positive offset to an aligned address must break alignment
